title: dianthus martuniensis
intro: Dianthus martuniensis, also known as the Armenian pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dianthus martuniensis include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems such as bronchitis and coughs. Dianthus martuniensis can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried flowers in hot water or as a tincture by extracting the plant's essential oils with a solvent.
